business_rules_deferred.reason: Following/followers relationship deferred to Sprint 3 (accounts module). Feed=following returns empty set with 200 as API convention instead of legacy 302 redirect.

technical_decisions.description: ARTICLES_PER_PAGE = 10 (corrects migration plan which incorrectly said 20). Authoritative source: legacy/apps/articles/views.py line 14.

UserStub model created as a temporary FK target for Article.authorId and Favorite.userId. Sprint 3 (accounts module) replaces this with the full User model. Same table name 'users' is used so the replacement is transparent.
BR-026 tags sidebar 5-minute cache (Django) not applicable to SPA. Tags are fetched on home page load (fresh from DB). No stale data concern as the SPA renders after HTTP roundtrip.
BR-029 HTMX partial responses (legacy used HTMX for feed switching) not applicable to REST+SPA architecture. The Angular frontend handles reactivity natively via signals.

BR-034: non-owner edit/delete returns 404 (not 403). Preserved from Django's get_object_or_404(Article, slug=slug, author=request.user) pattern — security by obscurity is intentional here (don't reveal slug ownership).

Fastify plugin NOT wrapped with fastify-plugin(fp) to preserve route prefix scoping. The authenticate decorator is available because authenticatePlugin IS fp-wrapped, decorating the root scope visible to all child scopes.
Article.slug allowNull:true in Sequelize column definition because Sequelize validates constraints BEFORE running beforeCreate hooks. The hook always sets slug; DB unique constraint still enforced.

articles.routes.ts imports from ./models barrel (index.ts) instead of individual model files. This ensures Sequelize associations (hasMany, belongsTo, belongsToMany) are wired before the first query execution.

warnings: GET /api/tags endpoint not yet implemented. ArticlesService.getTags() references it. Tags are returned per-article in GET /api/articles responses. A dedicated /api/tags endpoint should be added (Sprint 2 follow-up or Sprint 3).
Sprint 3 must swap UserStub references in models/index.ts with the real User model. The stub has no password/email fields — adequate for articles FK but not for auth.
notes: Translation iso-functional. All 23 test cases pass covering all business rules. Angular build succeeds. TypeScript strict mode compliant.
